title: scirpus maritimus subsp. tabernaemontani
intro: Scirpus maritimus subsp. tabernaemontani, also known as sea club-rush, is a plant species belonging to the sedge family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, alleviating urinary tract issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Scirpus maritimus subsp. tabernaemontani is often used in infusions, tinctures, and decoctions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
